extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each category: I counted only the “Against” rows, namely 2 criminal proceedings against Directors and 1 criminal proceeding against the Promoter, for 3 criminal proceedings; the Company, Subsidiaries, Key Managerial Personnel and Senior Management had nil against them. Statutory or regulatory proceedings against all listed entities were nil, and tax proceedings against the Company and Subsidiaries were nil; the “By Subsidiaries” criminal matter and its ₹0.41 million amount were excluded, as were the quantified matters against Directors and the Promoter because they are personal matters. No contingent-liabilities table or total is disclosed in the provided risk section. The company-wide top-five and top-ten customer concentrations are for Fiscal 2025 and are measured as percentages of revenue from operations: 29.76% and 40.78%, respectively; segment-only concentrations were not used. Litigation amounts are presented in ₹ million, and no quantified amount against the Company or Subsidiaries is disclosed, so no conversion to crore is applicable for the aggregate litigation amount.
